campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they remembered K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who recently passed away. The council also approved an updated beekeeping ordinance, discussed a state bill about development near transit hubs, and reviewed a study about homeless services in the West Valley region.
keyTopics: Memorial for K9 Lucas, police department's first working dog
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
Senate Bill 79 hearing on transit-oriented development (three council members had to step aside due to property conflicts)
West Valley Homeless Services feasibility study recommending regional task force

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a special meeting to review and approve work plans for five different city commissions that handle everything from parks and recreation to traffic safety and historic preservation. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year. The council also held a private session to discuss a legal case the city is considering pursuing.
keyTopics: Heritage Preservation Commission priorities for historic sites
Library and Public Art Commission plans for community art and library services
Parks and Recreation Commission goals for facilities and programs
Traffic Safety Commission work on road safety and traffic issues
Planning Commission development and land-use priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine administrative items like approving meeting minutes and reviewing the city's finances. The council also adopted policies on weed control, affordable housing unit transfers, and approved raising two new flags at city hall to recognize Progress Pride and Juneteenth celebrations.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion and final report
Affordable housing unit transfer exemption policy
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority update
Housing element implementation planning for upcoming year

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and check progress on housing and general planning goals. The council also approved a new residential development project (Solana Project) and finalized plans for community health and senior services for the year.
keyTopics: Approved the Solana Project residential development (Tract 10657)
Reviewed January 2026 financial reports and investments
Checked progress on housing and General Plan goals
Approved 2026 community health and senior services programs
Updated council meeting minute-taking procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to prepare for upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for the April 7 meeting, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked draft items for the April 14 meeting. The council also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized April 7 council meeting agenda
Set time slots for specific agenda items
Reviewed draft items for April 14 meeting
Checked public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings
Contact Recreation Division at 650-903-6331 for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development programs in Sunnyvale. The group approved minutes from their previous meeting and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for affordable housing projects
Community development programs seeking city support
Review and approval of February commission meeting records

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed through several new initiatives. They discussed forgiving past business license fees for companies that sign up through an amnesty program, promoting shopping at local stores during a summer campaign, and offering financial incentives to attract new retail shops and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - allowing businesses to catch up on fees without penalties
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to encourage residents to support Cupertino businesses
New incentives and tax breaks for retail stores and startups moving to the city
Outreach to shopping centers to increase participation in city programs
Public-Private Partnerships for funding city events and celebrations

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed matters related to historic buildings and landmarks in the community. Residents could attend in person or join online via Zoom to participate in the discussion and submit their opinions.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline: 12 p.m. day of meeting via email
Discussion of historic buildings and landmarks preservation

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative business, including approving previous meeting minutes and reviewing the schedule for upcoming meetings. The council also approved travel for Mayor Montano to attend an annual conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment.
keyTopics: Approved previous City Council meeting minutes
Mayor Montano attending WELL 2026 Annual Conference
New Milpitas Unified School District representative appointment to Economic committee
Review of upcoming agenda items and councilmember requests

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine items including officially recognizing March as Women's History Month. The council approved agreements with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies and authorized a three-year contract to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report additions to agenda
